title: King's Edge deliverable measure schema
description: Canonical authoring schema for benefit-realisation measures. Each measure is authored once at deliverable level and linked to one or more benefits through supportsBenefits.

measureType.description: Type of evidence, for example usage, qualitative judgement, adoption, quality assurance or quantitative benefit-realisation indicator.

supportsBenefits.description: Benefit IDs whose realisation this measure tests.

questionAnswered.description: The benefit-realisation question this measure answers.

measure.description: What evidence will be gathered, counted or judged. It should test whether value is being realised, not only whether delivery activity occurred.

target.description: Target, threshold or qualitative success standard. Use a judgement-based threshold where a numerical target would create false precision.

examples.title: Evidence is useful, fair and responsibly interpreted
examples.questionAnswered: Do programme teams find the evidence useful, fair and interpretable?
examples.measure: Structured programme-team feedback and sample review of profiles and story materials for clarity, caveat use and responsible interpretation.
examples.target: Programme teams judge the evidence usable and fair; sampled materials are appropriately caveated and avoid ranking or punitive comparison.
